name: Shepards Pie
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Cold, leftover roast lamb, beef, or veal
recipeInstructions:
1. Mince the cold meat, removing all fat, skin, and gristle first.
2. Cook potatoes, peeled and cut in halves, until soft enough for mashing.
3. Melt 1 tablespoon butter or margarine in a saucepan and stir in the flour.
4. Gradually add 1/4 pint (1 cup) meat stock or milk to the mixture, stirring constantly.
5. Continue adding until smooth and slightly thickened, then gently bring to a boil.
6. Stir in the minced meat over low heat for 3 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper to taste.
7. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
8. Mash the potatoes, season, and stir in 1/2 teaspoon (1/4 stick) butter or margarine, a little milk, and mix until smooth.
9. Set aside until the minced meat is cool enough to form a solid surface on the dish.
10. Spread the mashed potatoes about an inch thick over the cooled minced meat.
11. Dot with pieces of butter or cover with grated cheese, if desired.
12. Bake in a 400°F (200°C) oven for 30 minutes.
This dish may be prepared in the morning and left to cool before cooking in the evening without harm. Cabbage is an excellent vegetable to accompany this dish.
